title: Anesthesia at Remote Locations MCQ – Chapter 37
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav

questions.question: What chronic condition do patients at high altitudes (low barometric pressure) have?

options.B: Chronic hypoxia

questions.explanation: The patients at high altitudes (low barometric pressure) have chronic hypoxia which can lead to polycythemia, pulmonary hypertension, decreased pCO2 and lower bicarbonate concentration.

questions.question: What complications can chronic hypoxia at high altitude lead to?

options.B: Polycythemia, pulmonary hypertension, decreased pCO2 and lower bicarbonate concentration

questions.explanation: Chronic hypoxia can lead to polycythemia, pulmonary hypertension, decreased pCO2 (due to hyperventilation) and lower bicarbonate concentration (as a compensatory mechanism to low pCO2).

questions.question: What happens to the potency of anesthetic gases at high altitudes?

options.B: Decreases (proportional to partial pressure which decreases at high altitude)

questions.explanation: Potency of anesthetic gases is proportional to their partial pressure. At high altitudes where barometric pressure is decreased, the potency also decreases.

questions.question: At what altitude is the potency of nitrous oxide reduced to 50%?

options.B: More than 3,300 meters

questions.explanation: The potency of nitrous oxide is reduced to 50% at altitudes >3,300 meters.

questions.question: At what altitude should nitrous oxide not be used?

options.B: More than 2,000 meters

questions.explanation: It is recommended that nitrous oxide should not be used at altitudes more than 2,000 meters.

questions.question: What happens to the density of gases at high altitudes and how does it affect the rotameter?

options.B: Density decreases, rotameter under reads the actual flow rate

questions.explanation: Density of gases decreases at high altitudes, therefore, rotameter under reads the actual flow rate and this error may be up to 4 liters/minute.

questions.question: By how much can the rotameter error be at high altitudes?

options.B: Up to 4 liters/minute

questions.explanation: The rotameter under reads the actual flow rate and this error may be up to 4 liters/minute.

questions.question: At altered barometric pressure, do all vaporizers deliver fixed partial pressure of vapor?

options.B: All except desflurane deliver fixed partial pressure

questions.explanation: At altered barometric pressure, all vaporizers (except desflurane) deliver fixed partial pressure of vapor. Therefore, vaporizer settings need not be changed at low or high barometric pressure except for desflurane.

questions.question: Which vaporizer needs upward adjustment at high altitude?

options.B: Desflurane

questions.explanation: Vaporizer settings need not be changed at low or high barometric pressure except for desflurane, which needs an upward adjustment at high altitude.

questions.question: Why do Venturi type devices deliver higher oxygen at high altitude?

options.B: Because of decreased density and resistance

questions.explanation: Venturi type devices deliver higher oxygen (because of decreased density and resistance).

questions.question: Why is blood loss during surgery more at high altitude?

options.B: Due to high venous pressure and blood volume

questions.explanation: Blood loss during surgery is more due to high venous pressure and blood volume.

questions.question: Why are chances of postoperative hypoxemia high at high altitude?

options.B: Due to low ambient partial pressure of oxygen

questions.explanation: In the postoperative period, chances of hypoxemia are high due to low ambient partial pressure of oxygen.

questions.question: What is the incidence of postspinal headache at high altitude under regional anesthesia?

options.B: High (due to increased CSF pressure)

questions.explanation: The incidence of postspinal headache is high due to increased CSF pressure.

questions.question: In hyperbaric chamber, what can happen to tissues with nitrous oxide?

options.B: Tissues can become supersaturated causing significant diffusion hypoxia

questions.explanation: Tissues can become supersaturated with nitrous oxide causing significant diffusion hypoxia.

questions.question: Why should nitrous oxide not be used in helium atmosphere?

options.B: The effect of supersaturation is more evident in helium atmosphere

questions.explanation: The effect of supersaturation is more evident in helium atmosphere, therefore nitrous oxide should not be used in helium atmosphere.

questions.question: Why does the rotameter falsely give high values in hyperbaric chamber?

options.B: Due to increased gas density

questions.explanation: Rotameter will falsely give high values due to increased gas density.

questions.question: What should the endotracheal tube cuff be filled with in hyperbaric chamber?

options.B: Water or saline

questions.explanation: Endotracheal tube cuff should be filled with water or saline.

questions.question: What happens to the vaporizer output in hyperbaric chamber?

options.B: Becomes less

questions.explanation: The vaporizer output becomes less.

questions.question: Is regional anesthesia safe in hyperbaric chamber?

options.B: Yes, regional anesthesia is safe

questions.explanation: Regional anesthesia is safe.

questions.question: What does anesthesia outside operating theater mean?

options.B: All procedures done outside the operation theater (radiology, MRI, CT scan, gastroscopy, cardiology, neurology etc.)

questions.explanation: Nonoperating room anesthesia means all procedures done outside the operation theater. For example, anesthesia services utilized in radiology (MRI, CT scan), gastroscopy suites, intervention cardiology and neurology, etc.

questions.question: What are the challenges of procedure rooms outside OT?

options.B: Small and crowded, making placement of anesthesia machine, drug trolley, emergency cart and patient access difficult

questions.explanation: Procedure rooms are often small and crowded making placement of anesthesia machine, drug trolley, emergency cart and patient access difficult.

questions.question: What equipment-related challenge exists in nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: Nonavailability of adequate equipment, monitors, technical support and help in case of emergency

questions.explanation: Nonavailability of adequate equipment, monitors, technical support and help in case of emergency.

questions.question: In MRI suites, why does the anesthetist stand far away from the patient?

options.B: Due to the magnetic field (MRI suites require special non-ferrous equipment)

questions.explanation: Many times, like MRI suites, anesthetist stands far away from the patient.

questions.question: Why do medical proceduralists sometimes view anesthesia demand as obstructive?

options.B: Unlike surgeons, many medical proceduralists do not understand the intricacies and importance of anesthesia

questions.explanation: Unlike surgeons, many of the medical proceduralists do not understand the intricacies and importance of anesthesia. Any demand by anesthetist is often considered as obstructive by medical specialist.

questions.question: How do patients undergoing medical procedures compare to surgical patients in terms of health status?

options.B: They are often even sicker than surgical patients and seldom evaluated and medically optimized preoperatively

questions.explanation: Majority of the patients undergoing medical procedures are even sicker than surgical patients. In spite of that, they are seldom evaluated and medically optimized in preoperative period.

questions.question: What is the risk associated with radiology procedures requiring anesthesia?

options.B: Risk of exposure to radiation

questions.explanation: Risk of exposure to radiation.

questions.question: What complication can occur in radiology procedures?

options.B: Contrast-related anaphylaxis

questions.explanation: Contrast-related anaphylaxis can occur in radiology procedures.

questions.question: Why may patients for nonoperating room procedures not be adequately fasting?

options.B: Cases often come as unbooked, disturbing the schedule, and patients may not be adequately fasting

questions.explanation: Majority of the times, cases come as unbooked and so, disturbing the schedule. Patients may not be adequately fasting.

questions.question: What is the full fasting recommendation for nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: 6 hours for light food, 8 hours for full meal, and 2 hours for water

questions.explanation: Full fasting recommendation, i.e. 6 hours for light food, 8 hours for a full meal, and 2 hours for water is mandatory.

questions.question: Why is inadequate sedation a concern in nonoperating room procedures?

options.B: It can lead to conversion to general anesthesia, so full fasting is mandatory

questions.explanation: Inadequate sedation leading to conversion to general anesthesia is very common, therefore, full fasting recommendation is mandatory.

questions.question: What monitoring is highly desirable for spontaneously breathing patients in nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: Capnography (nasal capnography for spontaneously breathing patients) - can pick up life-threatening complications very early

questions.explanation: Other than pulse oximetry, ECG and BP, capnography (nasal capnography for spontaneously breathing patients) is highly desirable; capnography can pick up life-threatening complications very early.

questions.question: What anesthesia technique is most often used for nonoperating room procedures?

options.B: Either sedation with midazolam or total intravenous anesthesia (TIVA) with Propofol + Remifentanil

questions.explanation: Most often either sedation with midazolam or total intravenous anesthesia (TIVA) with Propofol + Remifentanil is utilized for nonoperating room procedures.

questions.question: In India, what is used instead of Remifentanil for TIVA in nonoperating room procedures?

options.B: Fentanyl (due to nonavailability of remifentanil)

questions.explanation: In India fentanyl, due to nonavailability of remifentanil, is utilized for nonoperating room procedures.

questions.question: What must all equipment in MRI suites be made of?

options.B: Aluminum (MRI compatible, nonferrous)

questions.explanation: For MRI suites, everything including anesthesia machine, monitors, oxygen cylinders must be MRI compatible, i.e. nonferrous. The equipments used in MRI suites are made up of aluminum.

questions.question: Why is nonoperating room anesthesia considered very challenging?

options.B: Due to small crowded space, inadequate equipment, inadequate preoperative evaluation, and inadequate knowledge of anesthesia intricacies by proceduralists

questions.explanation: Small and crowded space, nonavailability of adequate equipment, monitors, technical support and help in case of emergency, inadequate preoperative evaluation of patients and inadequate knowledge of anesthesia intricacies by medical proceduralists make nonoperating room anesthesia very challenging.

questions.question: Is nonoperating room anesthesia preferred by most anesthetists?

options.B: No, it is the least preferred by majority of anesthetists

questions.explanation: Nonoperating room anesthesia is very challenging and least preferred by majority of anesthetists.

questions.question: What is the compensation for anesthetists working outside OT compared to surgical cases?

options.B: Almost always undercompensated financially compared to surgical cases

questions.explanation: In spite of the high risk and adverse working circumstances, anesthetists are almost always undercompensated financially as compared to surgical cases.

questions.question: Why is pCO2 decreased at high altitude?

options.B: Due to hyperventilation (compensatory response to chronic hypoxia)

questions.explanation: Decreased pCO2 is due to hyperventilation which is a compensatory response to chronic hypoxia at high altitude.

questions.question: Why is bicarbonate concentration lower at high altitude?

options.B: As a compensatory mechanism to low pCO2 (renal compensation for respiratory alkalosis)
options.D: Due to increased renal excretion of CO2

questions.explanation: Lower bicarbonate concentration is a compensatory mechanism to low pCO2.

questions.question: At what altitude is it recommended to not use nitrous oxide?

options.B: More than 2,000 meters

questions.explanation: It is recommended that nitrous oxide should not be used at altitudes more than 2,000 meters.

questions.question: What is the effect of high altitude on pulmonary circulation?

options.B: Pulmonary hypertension

questions.explanation: Chronic hypoxia at high altitude can lead to pulmonary hypertension.

questions.question: What happens to all vaporizers except desflurane at altered barometric pressure?

options.B: They deliver fixed partial pressure of vapor so settings need not be changed

questions.explanation: At altered barometric pressure, all vaporizers (except desflurane) deliver fixed partial pressure of vapor. Therefore, vaporizer settings need not be changed.

questions.question: What is the fasting duration for water before nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: 2 hours

questions.explanation: Full fasting recommendation: 6 hours for light food, 8 hours for a full meal, and 2 hours for water.

questions.question: What is the fasting duration for a full meal before nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: 8 hours

questions.explanation: Full fasting recommendation: 8 hours for a full meal.

questions.question: What is the fasting duration for light food before nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: 6 hours

questions.explanation: Full fasting recommendation: 6 hours for light food.

questions.question: Can capnography detect life-threatening complications early in nonoperating room anesthesia?

options.B: Yes, capnography can pick up life-threatening complications very early

questions.explanation: Capnography can pick up life-threatening complications very early. It is highly desirable for nonoperating room anesthesia.

questions.question: Why must oxygen cylinders in MRI suites be made of aluminum?

options.B: They must be MRI compatible (nonferrous) - ferrous materials are attracted by the magnetic field

questions.explanation: For MRI suites, everything including oxygen cylinders must be MRI compatible, i.e. nonferrous. The equipments used in MRI suites are made up of aluminum.

questions.question: What type of polycythemia develops at high altitude?

options.B: Secondary polycythemia (due to chronic hypoxia stimulating erythropoietin)

questions.explanation: Chronic hypoxia at high altitude leads to polycythemia as a compensatory mechanism to increase oxygen carrying capacity.

questions.question: What is the main reason nonoperating room anesthesia cases are challenging regarding scheduling?

options.B: Majority of cases come as unbooked, disturbing the schedule

questions.explanation: Majority of the times, cases come as unbooked and so, disturbing the schedule.

questions.question: Are full fasting recommendations required for non-operating room anesthesia?

options.B: Yes, full fasting recommendations are required

questions.explanation: Full fasting recommendations are required for non-operating room anesthesia.
